Claude/fix early substitution bug aymt i (#202)

* Fix early substitution bug: don't persist user's team auto-subs in live match

When a player on the user's team was going to get injured during a match,
the auto-substitution generated by the simulator was being stored in the
database before the live match started. This caused the substitution to
appear at the beginning of the match in the UI.

The fix filters out auto-substitution events for the user's team in the
deferred (live) match across three methods:
- bulkInsertMatchEvents: don't store the sub event in match_events
- recordAutoSubstitutions: don't record it in the match's substitutions JSON
- bulkUpdateAppearances: don't credit the auto-sub-in player with an appearance

The simulator still generates these events internally for accurate team
strength calculation, but they are no longer persisted. The user will see
the injury event during the live match and can make their own substitution.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JgRvVrnpTA1ArLXB3XDMti

* Refactor: prevent user auto-subs at simulation time instead of filtering after

Instead of generating auto-substitutions for the user's team and then
filtering them out in three places within MatchResultProcessor, simply
don't pass bench players for the user's team to the simulator. The
simulator already guards with `$benchPlayers !== null`, so injury events
are still generated but no substitution follows.

This removes all filtering logic from MatchResultProcessor (reverts three
methods to their original signatures) and moves the fix to a single
point in MatchdayOrchestrator::simulateMatch().

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JgRvVrnpTA1ArLXB3XDMti

* security(config,test): UnicodeError親クラス化とNULバイトTypeError対策でSSRF保護を完全化

commit.message:
Fix mel spectrogram hop size and add classification debug logging (#276)

The essentia.js EssentiaTFInputExtractor.computeFrameWise() defaults
hop size to frameSize (512) when not provided. The MIN_AUDIO_DURATION
check assumed hop=256, so audio between 2.1s–4.1s passed the guard
but produced 0 patches in the worker, causing "Audio too short" errors.

Fix: pass hop=256 explicitly to computeFrameWise().

Also adds detailed debug logging to the worker message handler and
classify pipeline, and improves user-facing error messages to indicate
audio may be too short.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01XKRuUHqSKJj9TES9S8ydX1
